SWS AI CONFIDENTIAL – INTERNAL USE ONLY
CAREER DEVELOPMENT
Benefits & Compensation
Salary Structure
The SWS AI compensation package consists of:
- Basic Salary: 40% of CTC
- House Rent Allowance (HRA): 20% of Basic
- Special Allowance: Variable component
- Performance Bonus: Up to 15% of annual CTC based on performance rating
- Variable Pay: Project-linked incentives for eligible roles
Health Insurance

Health Insurance
All full-time employees are covered under a Group Health Insurance policy of INR 5,00,000 per annum.
Coverage extends to the employee's spouse and up to 2 dependent children. Premium is fully borne by SWS
AI.
Provident Fund
SWS AI contributes 12% of Basic Salary to the Employee Provident Fund (EPF) as per statutory
requirements. Employees contribute an equal 12%. PF accounts are managed through the EPFO portal.
Learning & Development

Learning & Development
SWS AI provides an annual L&D budget of INR 20,000 per employee for courses, certifications, books, and
conferences. Courses must be pre-approved by the manager and HR.
Stock Options
Senior engineers and above are eligible for Employee Stock Option Plans (ESOPs) based on performance
and tenure. Details are shared during offer negotiation.
Referral Bonus
Employees who refer successful candidates receive a referral bonus:
- Junior roles (0-3 years): INR 15,000

- Junior roles (0-3 years): INR 15,000
- Mid-level roles (3-6 years): INR 25,000
- Senior roles (6+ years): INR 40,000
Bonus is paid after the referred employee completes 6 months.
Internet & Equipment
All full-time employees receive a company-issued laptop. A one-time equipment allowance of INR 10,000 is
provided for peripherals. Internet reimbursement is not offered under standard policy.
Leave Encashment

Leave Encashment
Earned leave can be encashed at the time of resignation or at year-end (subject to a minimum balance of 5
days). Leave encashment is calculated on Basic + DA salary.
SWS AI | Benefits & Compensation Page 1 of 1

SWS AI CONFIDENTIAL – INTERNAL USE ONLY
WORKPLACE STANDARDS
Code of Conduct
Introduction
SWS AI expects all employees to conduct themselves with integrity, respect, and professionalism at all times
— whether in the office, working remotely, or representing the company externally.
Professional Behavior
Employees must:
- Treat all colleagues, clients, and vendors with respect.
- Maintain confidentiality of sensitive business information.

- Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose any potential conflicts to HR.
- Represent the company honestly and professionally in all external communications.
Anti-Harassment Policy
SWS AI has a zero-tolerance policy for harassment of any kind — physical, verbal, sexual, or psychological.
Any form of bullying, intimidation, or hostile behavior is strictly prohibited. Violations will be investigated and
may result in termination.
Social Media Policy

may result in termination.
Social Media Policy
Employees must not post confidential company information, product roadmaps, client data, or internal
communications on any public platform. Personal opinions shared online must not be linked to SWS AI
without explicit approval.
Conflict of Interest
Employees must avoid situations where personal interests conflict with the interests of SWS AI. This includes

taking up freelance work for competitors, accepting gifts from clients beyond a nominal value, or making
decisions influenced by personal gain.
Intellectual Property
All work products, code, designs, and ideas created during employment are the intellectual property of SWS
AI. Employees must not share proprietary code, algorithms, or product information outside the company.
Reporting Violations
Employees who witness or experience a violation of this Code of Conduct must report it to HR or their

manager immediately. Anonymous reporting mechanisms are available. Retaliation against anyone who
reports in good faith is strictly prohibited.
SWS AI | Code of Conduct Page 1 of 1

SWS AI CONFIDENTIAL – INTERNAL USE ONLY
ABOUT SWS AI
Company Overview & Mission
About SWS AI
SWS AI is a technology company specializing in intelligent automation, AI-powered products, and custom
software solutions. Founded with a mission to democratize artificial intelligence for businesses of all sizes,
SWS AI builds cutting-edge tools that help organizations work smarter.
Our Mission
To harness the power of artificial intelligence and modern software engineering to create products that solve

real-world problems and drive measurable outcomes for our clients and partners.
Our Vision
To be a globally recognized AI-first technology company that sets new benchmarks in intelligent software
solutions by 2030.
Core Values
1. Innovation First – We prioritize experimentation and bold thinking.
2. Quality Without Compromise – Every product we ship reflects our commitment to excellence.
3. People Over Process – We invest in our team's growth and well-being.

4. Transparency – Honest communication with clients, partners, and teammates.
5. Customer Obsession – Success is measured by our customers' success.
What We Build
SWS AI develops AI-powered SaaS platforms, enterprise automation tools, mobile applications, and custom
software solutions for clients across healthcare, fintech, retail, and logistics sectors.
Team & Culture
SWS AI is a remote-friendly organization with offices in Chennai, India. We believe in flat hierarchies, open

communication, and continuous learning. Every team member is encouraged to contribute ideas and take
ownership.
Contact
Email: hr@swsai.in | Website: www.swsai.in | LinkedIn: linkedin.com/company/sws-ai
SWS AI | Company Overview & Mission Page 1 of 1

SWS AI CONFIDENTIAL – INTERNAL USE ONLY
HUMAN RESOURCES
HR Policy
Purpose
This HR Policy outlines the principles, standards, and procedures that govern the employment relationship at
SWS AI. All employees are expected to read, understand, and adhere to these guidelines.
Working Hours
Standard working hours are 9:30 AM to 6:30 PM, Monday through Friday. Employees are expected to
maintain a minimum of 9 productive hours per day. Flexible start and end times may be arranged with prior

manager approval.
Attendance & Punctuality
Employees must mark attendance daily via the company HR portal. Absences must be reported to the
manager and HR team at least one hour before the start of the workday. Repeated unexplained absences
may result in disciplinary action.
Code of Professionalism
All employees must maintain a professional demeanor in all interactions — internal and external. This

includes respectful communication, appropriate attire (during video calls), and adherence to meeting
protocols.
Grievance Redressal
Employees with workplace concerns should raise them with their direct manager first. If unresolved, issues
may be escalated to HR. SWS AI maintains a zero-tolerance policy for harassment, discrimination, and
bullying.
Performance Management
Employee performance is reviewed bi-annually. Reviews assess technical competency, collaboration,

ownership, and growth mindset. Consistent underperformance will trigger a Performance Improvement Plan
(PIP).
Disciplinary Action
Violations of company policy may result in verbal warnings, written warnings, suspension, or termination,
depending on severity. Serious misconduct — including data theft or harassment — may result in immediate
termination.
Equal Opportunity
SWS AI is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, age, religion,

disability, sexual orientation, or nationality.
SWS AI | HR Policy Page 1 of 1

SWS AI CONFIDENTIAL – INTERNAL USE ONLY
COMPLIANCE
IT & Security Policy
Purpose
This policy establishes guidelines for acceptable use of SWS AI's IT systems, data security practices, and
incident response procedures. All employees must comply with this policy from Day 1.
Acceptable Use of IT Resources
Company-issued devices and systems must be used primarily for work purposes. Limited personal use is
tolerated but must not:
- Involve illegal activities.
- Compromise device security.

- Compromise device security.
- Consume excessive bandwidth.
- Include installation of unauthorized software.
Password Policy
All passwords must:
- Be at least 12 characters long.
- Include uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters.
- Be unique across different systems.
- Be changed every 90 days.
- Never be shared with colleagues.
Use the company-approved password manager (1Password) for storage.
Data Classification
SWS AI data is classified into 4 levels:

SWS AI data is classified into 4 levels:
1. Public – Can be shared freely (marketing materials, blog posts).
2. Internal – For employees only (policies, general communications).
3. Confidential – Limited access (client data, source code, financial data).
4. Restricted – Highly sensitive (credentials, PII, trade secrets).
Always handle data according to its classification level.
Device Security
All company devices must:
- Have full-disk encryption enabled.

- Have full-disk encryption enabled.
- Run the latest OS and security patches.
- Have the company endpoint security software installed.
- Use screen lock (max 5-minute timeout).
- Never have unauthorized USB devices connected.
SWS AI | IT & Security Policy Page 1 of 2

Access Control
Access to systems and data is granted on a need-to-know basis. Employees must:
- Not share login credentials with anyone.
- Not use another employee's credentials.
- Report lost or stolen devices immediately.
- Request access revocation for colleagues who have left the company.
Incident Response
If you suspect a security incident:
1. Do not panic or try to fix it yourself.
2. Immediately notify your manager and IT security team.
3. Preserve evidence — do not delete logs or files.

4. Document what you observed (time, what happened, affected systems).
5. Cooperate fully with the IT investigation.
Cloud & Data Storage
All work files must be stored in company-approved cloud storage (Google Drive / AWS S3). Personal cloud
storage (personal Dropbox, iCloud, etc.) must not contain company data. Sensitive data must never be
emailed externally without encryption.
Third-Party Tools

Third-Party Tools
Installation of third-party software or browser extensions on company devices requires prior IT approval.
Unapproved software may introduce vulnerabilities and will be removed remotely.
SWS AI | IT & Security Policy Page 2 of 2

SWS AI CONFIDENTIAL – INTERNAL USE ONLY
HUMAN RESOURCES
Leave Policy
Overview
SWS AI provides a structured leave policy to ensure employees can maintain work-life balance while the
organization meets its operational needs. All leaves must be applied for in advance through the HR portal,
except in emergencies.
Annual / Earned Leave (EL)
Employees are entitled to 18 days of earned leave per year, accrued at 1.5 days per month. Unused EL can

be carried forward up to a maximum of 30 days. Any unused leave beyond 30 days will lapse.
Sick Leave (SL)
Employees are entitled to 10 days of sick leave per year. Sick leave cannot be carried forward. Medical
certificates are required for sick leave exceeding 3 consecutive days.
Casual Leave (CL)
Employees are entitled to 6 casual leave days per year. CL cannot be carried forward or encashed. A
maximum of 2 consecutive days can be taken as casual leave at a time.
Maternity Leave

Maternity Leave
Female employees are entitled to 26 weeks of paid maternity leave as per the Maternity Benefit Act, 1961.
Additional unpaid leave of up to 8 weeks may be granted upon request.
Paternity Leave
Male employees are entitled to 5 days of paid paternity leave, to be availed within 3 months of childbirth or
adoption.
Compensatory Off (Comp-Off)
Employees who work on weekends or public holidays may be eligible for compensatory off. Comp-off must

be availed within 30 days of the extra day worked and requires manager approval.
Public Holidays
SWS AI observes a list of public holidays published at the beginning of each calendar year. The list includes
national holidays and select regional holidays relevant to office locations.
Leave Without Pay (LWP)
Leave without pay may be granted at the discretion of HR and management when an employee has
exhausted all other leave types. LWP impacts variable pay and annual increments.
SWS AI | Leave Policy Page 1 of 2

Leave Application Process
All leaves must be applied for via the HR portal. Emergency leaves must be communicated via call or
message to the manager, followed by a formal application in the portal within 24 hours.
SWS AI | Leave Policy Page 2 of 2

SWS AI CONFIDENTIAL – INTERNAL USE ONLY
GETTING STARTED
Employee Onboarding Guide
Welcome to SWS AI
Congratulations on joining the SWS AI family! This guide will help you get set up, understand your first-week
schedule, and find everything you need to hit the ground running.
Before Day 1
Before your first day:
- Complete all documentation sent by HR (offer letter, NDAs, tax forms).
- Set up your company email account using the credentials shared by IT.

- Install required software: Slack, Zoom, Jira, GitHub, VS Code.
- Read the Code of Conduct and HR Policy documents.
Day 1 Schedule
9:30 AM – Welcome meeting with HR
10:00 AM – Office/team tour (virtual walkthrough for remote employees)
11:00 AM – Introduction to your direct manager
12:00 PM – Lunch with the team
2:00 PM – IT setup and tools walkthrough
4:00 PM – Company overview presentation
5:30 PM – Q&A session
First Week Goals
By the end of your first week, you should:

By the end of your first week, you should:
- Have met all immediate team members.
- Have access to all required tools and systems.
- Understand your role's key responsibilities.
- Set up your development environment (for engineering roles).
- Complete mandatory compliance training in the HR portal.
Tools & Systems
At SWS AI we use:
- Communication: Slack
- Project Management: Jira
- Version Control: GitHub
- Documentation: Notion
- Video Calls: Google Meet / Zoom

- Video Calls: Google Meet / Zoom
- Cloud: AWS (primary), GCP (secondary)
- Code Reviews: GitHub Pull Requests
SWS AI | Employee Onboarding Guide Page 1 of 2

Your First 30 Days
The first 30 days focus on learning:
- Days 1-7: Orientation, tool setup, team introductions.
- Days 8-14: Deep dive into current projects and codebase.
- Days 15-21: First small tasks or bug fixes under guidance.
- Days 22-30: Begin contributing independently; first 1:1 with manager.
Getting Help
Don't hesitate to ask for help. Your assigned buddy will support you through the first month. You can also
reach out to:
- Your manager for work-related queries.

- Your manager for work-related queries.
- HR (hr@swsai.in) for policy-related questions.
- IT (it@swsai.in) for system access issues.
Key Contacts
CEO: Karthik Rajendran | CTO: Nithya Suresh | HR Head: Priya Menon | Engineering Lead: Arun Kumar |
Design Lead: Sneha Pillai
SWS AI | Employee Onboarding Guide Page 2 of 2

SWS AI CONFIDENTIAL – INTERNAL USE ONLY
CAREER DEVELOPMENT
Performance Review Policy
Overview
SWS AI conducts structured performance reviews to recognize employee contributions, identify growth areas,
and align individual goals with company objectives.
Review Cycles
Performance reviews are conducted twice a year:
- Mid-Year Review: July
- Annual Review: January
Probationary employees receive a review at the end of their 3-month probation period.
Review Framework

Review Framework
Performance is assessed across 5 dimensions:
1. Technical Competency – Skill level and quality of work.
2. Ownership & Accountability – Taking responsibility and delivering results.
3. Collaboration & Communication – Teamwork and cross-functional effectiveness.
4. Innovation & Problem Solving – Bringing creative solutions to challenges.
5. Growth Mindset – Commitment to continuous learning and improvement.
Rating Scale
Ratings are given on a 5-point scale:

Ratings are given on a 5-point scale:
5 – Exceptional: Consistently exceeds expectations.
4 – Strong: Frequently exceeds expectations.
3 – Meets Expectations: Consistently delivers what is expected.
2 – Needs Improvement: Partially meets expectations.
1 – Unsatisfactory: Consistently falls short of expectations.
Self-Assessment
Before each review cycle, employees complete a self-assessment form in the HR portal. This covers

accomplishments, challenges, areas for improvement, and goals for the next period.
Goal Setting
Following the review, employees and managers collaboratively set SMART goals (Specific, Measurable,
Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound) for the next half-year.
Performance Improvement Plans
Employees rated 1 or 2 will be placed on a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) with clearly defined
improvement targets and a 60-day timeline. Failure to meet PIP targets may result in role change or
termination.

termination.
SWS AI | Performance Review Policy Page 1 of 2

Promotions & Increments
Annual increments and promotions are tied to the Annual Review. Exceptional performers (rating 5) are
eligible for fast-track promotions. Increment percentages are determined by budget and performance rating.
SWS AI | Performance Review Policy Page 2 of 2

SWS AI CONFIDENTIAL – INTERNAL USE ONLY
HUMAN RESOURCES
Resignation & Exit Policy
Purpose
This policy governs the process for an employee's voluntary resignation and the exit procedures to be
followed. A smooth offboarding ensures continuity of work and a positive experience for the departing
employee.
Notice Period
All employees must serve a notice period as mentioned in their employment contract. The standard notice
period is:
- Probation: 2 weeks
- Post-confirmation (0-1 year): 1 month

- Post-confirmation (0-1 year): 1 month
- Post-confirmation (1+ years): 2 months
Early relieving may be granted based on project requirements and management discretion.
Resignation Process
1. Employee submits a formal resignation letter to their direct manager and HR via email.
2. HR acknowledges the resignation within 2 working days.
3. Last working day is calculated based on notice period.
4. Exit interview is scheduled with HR.
Notice Period Buyout

Notice Period Buyout
Employees may request a notice period buyout. The company may waive the remaining notice period on
receiving the buyout amount equivalent to the basic salary for the remaining days. This is subject to
management approval.
Knowledge Transfer
Departing employees must complete a structured knowledge transfer to their replacement or team. This
includes documentation, handover of ongoing projects, and access transfers.
Asset Return

Asset Return
All company assets — including laptops, access cards, and any company-owned equipment — must be
returned before the last working day. Full and final settlement will be processed only after asset clearance.
Full & Final Settlement
Full and final settlement including outstanding salary, earned leave encashment, and any other dues will be
processed within 30 days of the last working day, subject to clearance from IT, finance, and management.
Non-Disparagement

Non-Disparagement
Employees are expected to maintain confidentiality and not disparage the company, its products, employees,
or clients after departure. The NDA signed at the time of joining remains in force.
SWS AI | Resignation & Exit Policy Page 1 of 2

Rehire Eligibility
Employees who leave on good terms are eligible for rehire. Employees terminated for cause are generally not
eligible for rehire.
SWS AI | Resignation & Exit Policy Page 2 of 2

SWS AI CONFIDENTIAL – INTERNAL USE ONLY
WORKPLACE STANDARDS
Work From Home Policy
Overview
SWS AI supports a hybrid work model. Employees may work from home subject to manager approval, role
requirements, and project timelines. This policy sets expectations for productivity, communication, and
security while working remotely.
Eligibility
WFH is available to all full-time employees who have completed their probation period (3 months).

Employees on a PIP or those with attendance concerns may not be eligible for WFH.
WFH Days
Employees may work from home up to 3 days per week in a hybrid arrangement. Full-time WFH
arrangements require specific approval from the HR Head and are limited to exceptional circumstances.
Productivity Expectations
WFH employees must:
- Be available on all communication channels (Slack, email) during working hours.
- Attend all scheduled meetings with camera on.
- Meet daily standup commitments.

- Meet daily standup commitments.
- Deliver work on time as if working from the office.
Home Office Requirements
Employees working from home must have:
- A stable internet connection (minimum 50 Mbps).
- A dedicated quiet workspace.
- Company-issued or approved device with security software installed.
- A webcam and headset for video calls.
Security Requirements
When working from home, employees must:
- Use the company VPN at all times.

- Use the company VPN at all times.
- Not access company systems from public Wi-Fi without a VPN.
- Lock their computer when stepping away.
- Not share their screen or device with household members.
Expense Policy
SWS AI does not reimburse internet, electricity, or home office setup costs unless specifically pre-approved
by management. Company-issued equipment is provided to all full-time employees.
SWS AI | Work From Home Policy Page 1 of 1
